rulings.Case Title: KAZUKO K. ARTUS VS. GRAMERCY TOWERS CONDOMINIUM ASSOCATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Preliminary Injunction
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for December 6, 2022 line 1. PLAINTIFF KAZUKO ARTUS' NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION FOR PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION is continued to December 7, 2022. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: JOHN STEWART COMPANY VS. THE ESTATE OF DIANA CARTER-BIBBS ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for December 6, 2022 line 2. DEFENDANT JERRY FUQUA'S DEMURRER TO 4TH AMENDED COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED with final leave to amend (1) for Plaintiff to attach a complete legible copy of the Lease with any addenda/attachments in Plaintiff's possession or expressly allege that Plaintiff is not in possession of all or part of such document; or (2) if Plaintiff believes as suggested in opposition that the Lease is not relevant to Plaintiff's Unlawful Detainer, Plaintiff may omit the lease and establish the requisite elements of unlawful detainer against the moving defendant without referencing the lease. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: LAUREL GARDENS OF BETHEL AME VS. JOANNE JACKSON ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for December 6, 2022 line 3. DEFENDANT JOANNE JACKSON'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is OVERRULED 5 days to answer.
